timeline.label: Election Announcement
timeline.description: Election Commission of India announces the schedule for 18th Lok Sabha elections.

timeline.label: Model Code of Conduct
timeline.description: MCC restricts government from announcing new schemes, using official resources for campaigning. Ensures level playing field.

timeline.label: Final Voter List Published
timeline.description: The final electoral roll is published. No new names can be added after this date.

timeline.label: Nomination Filing (Phase 1)
timeline.description: Candidates file nomination papers with the Returning Officer along with a security deposit of ₹25,000 (₹12,500 for SC/ST).

timeline.label: Scrutiny of Nominations
timeline.description: Returning Officer checks if nomination papers are valid and candidates meet eligibility criteria.

timeline.label: Campaign Period
timeline.description: Candidates and parties hold rallies, distribute manifestos, run ads. Must follow ECI expenditure limits.

timeline.label: Polling (7 Phases)
timeline.description: Voting happens across 7 phases. Voters press button on EVM. VVPAT prints a slip for verification.

timeline.label: Vote Counting
timeline.description: Votes are counted under the supervision of Returning Officers and observers. Results declared constituency-wise.

timeline.label: Results Declared
timeline.description: Winners receive Certificate of Election. President invites the majority party/coalition to form the government.

timeline.label: Oath Ceremony
timeline.description: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers take oath administered by the President at Rashtrapati Bhavan.

timeline.label: Election Announcement
timeline.description: ECI announces Maharashtra assembly election schedule.

timeline.label: Nomination Filing
timeline.description: Candidates file nominations at district level.

timeline.label: Campaign Period
timeline.description: Active campaigning. No campaigning allowed 48 hours before polling (silent period).

timeline.label: Polling Day
timeline.description: Single phase voting across all 288 assembly constituencies.

timeline.label: SEC Notification
timeline.description: State Election Commission (SEC) — not ECI — conducts panchayat elections.

timeline.label: Voter List Preparation
timeline.description: Ward-wise voter lists prepared by local authorities.

timeline.label: Nomination & Withdrawal
timeline.description: Candidates from the village itself file nominations. No party symbols in most states.

timeline.label: Polling Day
timeline.description: Ballot papers often used instead of EVMs in some states for panchayat elections.

ECI.name: Election Commission of India
ECI.role: Constitutional body that conducts Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha and State Assembly elections.

SEC.name: State Election Commission
SEC.role: Conducts Panchayati Raj and Urban Local Body elections.
